original_image_tags: A newspaper clipping from The Washington Daily News, dated January 5, 1954. The clipping features two black-and-white photographs. The top photograph shows two men, identified in the caption as Pfc. Norman Viets (left) and Maj. D. D. Pomerleau, smiling. The bottom photograph is a profile shot of a man pointing with his right index finger. Handwritten annotations in blue ink are visible at the top and top right of the clipping.

elements.description: A vintage newspaper clipping from The Washington Daily News, dated January 5, 1954, featuring the headline 'Are They Hiding Those Lights Under a Bushel?' and two black-and-white photographs of three men. The clipping includes typed body text and is accompanied by several handwritten annotations, a stamped date 'JAN 12 1954', and the number '162-83894-A'.

judge.reason: Description B claims three men in the photographs while Description A describes only two men across two separate photographs, creating a direct contradiction in object count.
